mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over a 3-month window signals the onset of monetary easing, lowering the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This is an always-on trend effect independent of regime.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.26% in March 2007, near cycle peak
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS change and forward 20d SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls more than 10% from its trailing 12-month peak, it signals a material contraction in residential construction demand that historically precedes broader consumer demand weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days).
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 10% from trailing 12-month peak
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage_value 1525.0 in Feb 2007 down from prior peaks near 2000+ in 2005-06
failure_condition: In periods where the HOUST decline trigger fires, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive more than 55% of the time, or the effect disappears in walk-forward testing pre/post 2008 with Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deceleration in PAYEMS month-over-month growth combined with a rise in UNRATE indicates a turning labor market, a leading indicator of recession risk that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. Neither series alone is sufficient; both must move adversely together.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth falls below prior 6-month average AND UNRATE rises by >=0.2pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% and PAYEMS growth still positive in March 2007
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are met jointly, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of historically elevated FEDFUNDS (above 5%), a deceleration in INDPRO signals that tight monetary policy is beginning to constrain industrial output, foreshadowing a broader economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. In easing or low-rate regimes this relationship is expected to be muted or absent.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS held at 5.25-5.26% since mid-2006, restrictive relative to prior decade
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO momentum during FEDFUNDS>5% regimes fails to produce below-median forward 60d SPX returns, or the direction flips to positive in more than one out of three historical restrictive-rate episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer spending, which supports near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days. This is treated as an always-on short-horizon trend signal.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage_value 367539 million in Feb 2007 showing continued retail sales expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS 3-month growth and forward 5-day SPX return remains below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, indicating no persistent short-horizon predictive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 3% while FEDFUNDS remains elevated near cycle highs, markets price in stagflation risk and constrained policy flexibility, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as investors discount reduced central bank room to ease in a slowdown.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY change exceeds 3% while FEDFUNDS is above 5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised_value 204.226 in Feb 2007 vintage implying YoY near 2.4-3%, FEDFUNDS at 5.26%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns during trigger-active periods are positive more than 55% of the time, or Rank IC < 0.03 across historical high-inflation/high-rate episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle regime where UNRATE is at multi-year lows (below 4.5%), any deceleration in PAYEMS growth is interpreted by markets as an early recession signal rather than benign moderation, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This effect is expected to reverse or vanish in early-cycle recovery regimes where low unemployment coexists with accelerating hiring.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% in March 2007, near cycle low, historically associated with late-cycle peaks
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with historically low unemployment
failure_condition: PAYEMS deceleration during sub-4.5% UNRATE regimes fails to precede negative 20d SPX returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or Rank IC flips sign across different late-cycle episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in HOUST and persistently high FEDFUNDS jointly indicate that restrictive credit conditions are actively suppressing housing demand, a mechanism with historically large spillovers to consumer wealth and financials, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. Neither weak housing alone nor high rates alone is sufficient without the other.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines >5% over 6 months AND FEDFUNDS remains above 5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised_value 1480 thousand units in Feb 2007 declining while FEDFUNDS held at 5.26%
failure_condition: When both HOUST decline and FEDFUNDS>5% trigger jointly, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction effect Rank IC is not statistically distinguishable from the sum of individual single-series effects.
